subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Apply the Distributive Property to solve complex equations
Solve word problems with multiple conditions
Solve equations with variables on both sides
Create algebraic models for real-world geometric and financial problems

lesson_plan.title: Equations with Brackets and Multiple Steps
topics_covered: Order of operations in solving (Reverse PEMDAS)
teaching_strategy: Layer-by-layer peeling strategy (removing outer terms first)
assessment: Error analysis: 'Spot the mistake in this bracket expansion'

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Word Problems
student_activities: Solve: 'The sum of three consecutive integers is 51. Find the numbers.'
A father is 3 times as old as his son. In 12 years, he will be twice as old. Find their ages.

teacher_tip: Encourage students to define their variable clearly first (e.g., 'Let x be the son's age'). Half the battle in hard word problems is just setting up the variable!
